heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 545 Days: Approval Falls to 54%, Banxico Surprises with Rate Cut, IEEPA Tariffs Struck Down
heroSubtitle: President Sheinbaum's approval rating fell to 54% — the lowest of her presidency — driven by crime/corruption anxieties and economic headwinds. Banxico cut its benchmark rate by 25bp to 6.75% despite inflation surging to 4.63%, sending the peso to 18.12. The U.S. Supreme Court voided IEEPA tariffs, replacing them with a 10% Section 122 surcharge, while USMCA formal review talks launched March 16. Sheinbaum delivered water rights to Baja California, announced a national femicide law, and reaffirmed Mexico's Gulf of Mexico naming position amid renewed Trump pressure.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House
